description_long: This fast food restaurant is a two-story building with a steep, dark gray gabled roof and a prominent front porch entrance. The exterior walls are primarily light gray with large, grid-patterned windows on both floors, allowing for ample natural light inside. The front entrance features a covered awning supported by columns, leading up a short flight of stairs to the main door. The lower portion of the building is accented with a brick facade, adding a touch of warmth and contrast to the otherwise modern design. The roof includes a small chimney and subtle architectural details, while the side and rear views reveal additional windows and a secondary entrance. The overall structure is solid and substantial, typical of a standalone fast food restaurant in an urban or suburban setting.
description: A two-story fast food restaurant with a steep gabled roof, large grid windows, and a brick-accented lower facade. The building features a prominent front porch entrance and modern exterior details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the expansive dark gray roof, large grid windows, and the main entrance porch with brick steps.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the covered porch entrance, symmetrical window arrangement, and brick base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar window layout, visible secondary entrance, and roof details including a chimney.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the gabled roof's peak, a single window, and minimal architectural detailing.
attribute_architectural_style: Modern suburban fast food style with a steep gabled roof, large grid windows, brick accents, and a prominent covered entrance, blending contemporary and traditional elements.
